text.title: css-in-js-basics Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for css-in-js-basics as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using non-transient prop names (variant, size, color) in styled-components without the $ prefix.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: React forwards unknown props to the DOM, producing "Received `true` for a non-boolean attribute" warnings and invalid HTML. The fix requires either the $ prefix or explicit shouldForwardProp configuration.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without augmentation, theme is typed any inside template literals. Agents inline hex values (#FF0000) instead of consuming token variables (theme.colors.primary), breaking the design token contract and making theming changes difficult.

failure-mode.description: Copying Pages Router SSR setup (_document.tsx) into Next.js App Router.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: App Router does not use _document.tsx. Styled-components in App Router requires a 'use client' registry component wrapping the tree at the layout level, not a custom Document class.

failure-mode.description: LLMs default to non-transient props (variant instead of $variant); requires a lint rule or explicit prompt instruction.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without a rule enforcing the $ prefix, agents produce code like <Button variant="primary"> which leaks the prop to the DOM. Require explicit prop prefixing in the prompt and add an ESLint rule to catch it in review.

failure-mode.description: Agents copy SSR setup from Pages Router examples into App Router and break.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Pages Router uses _document.tsx. App Router needs a 'use client' registry component, not _document.tsx. Explicitly instruct agents which Next.js version is in use.

failure-mode.description: The theme parameter inside template literals is typed any without the augmentation file; agents reach for as any to silence errors.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without DefaultTheme augmentation, TypeScript cannot infer theme shape. Ensure the augmentation file is in place and that agents reference it in prompts.

failure-mode.description: Generated styled(Component) calls drop refs unless the inner component is forwardRef — agents rarely add the wrapper.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: styled-components cannot forward refs through wrapper components unless the inner component is explicitly forwardRef'd. Require agents to add forwardRef when styling custom components.

failure-mode.description: As polymorphism: agents type as: 'a' | 'button' but forget that styled-components actually accepts component types too; lose flexibility.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Styled-components as prop accepts not just string tags but component types. Agents miss this flexibility. Document in prompts that as: React.ElementType is the correct type.

failure-mode.description: Asked to "make this dynamic", LLMs add a styled.div inside the function body — every render creates a new component class.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: styled definitions inside render bodies cause re-mounts on every state change. Lint or block in review. Move styled definitions to module scope.
